questions.question: What is the chemical symbol for water?
questions.explanation: Water is H2O — two hydrogen atoms bonded to one oxygen atom.

questions.question: What is the closest planet to the Sun?
questions.explanation: Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun. The order is: Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune.

questions.question: What does a plant use to make food during photosynthesis?
questions.explanation: Plants take in carbon dioxide from air + water from roots + sunlight energy to make glucose (food) and release oxygen.

questions.question: Which type of rock is formed from cooled magma or lava?
questions.explanation: Igneous rock forms when magma (underground) or lava (above ground) cools and hardens. Granite and basalt are igneous rocks.

questions.question: What is the unit of electrical resistance?
questions.explanation: Electrical resistance is measured in Ohms (Ω), named after Georg Ohm. Ohm's Law: V = I × R.

questions.question: What is the function of the heart?
answers: To pump blood around the body
questions.explanation: The heart is a muscle that pumps blood throughout the circulatory system, delivering oxygen and nutrients to cells.

questions.question: What force pulls objects towards Earth?
questions.explanation: Gravity is the force that pulls all objects towards the centre of the Earth. It keeps us on the ground and the Moon in orbit.

questions.question: Which of these is a renewable energy source?
questions.explanation: Solar power uses energy from the sun, which is renewable. Coal, gas and oil are fossil fuels — they will eventually run out.

questions.question: What is the role of the lungs in the human body?
answers: To exchange oxygen and carbon dioxide
questions.explanation: The lungs take in oxygen from the air we breathe and transfer it into the blood, while removing carbon dioxide as waste.

questions.question: What is friction?
answers: A force that pulls objects together
A force that slows down moving objects by resisting motion
A force that pushes objects upward
The force of gravity on a surface
questions.explanation: Friction is a force that opposes motion between two surfaces. Tyres grip the road because of friction — without it, cars would skid.

questions.question: In a food chain, what is a producer?
answers: An animal that hunts other animals
A plant that makes its own food using sunlight
A decomposer that breaks down dead matter
questions.explanation: Producers (plants) use photosynthesis to make food from sunlight. They are always at the start of a food chain.

questions.question: What causes day and night on Earth?
answers: Earth orbiting the Sun once a year
Earth spinning on its own axis every 24 hours
The Sun moving around the Earth
questions.explanation: Earth spins on its axis once every 24 hours. The side facing the Sun has day, while the side facing away has night.

questions.question: What do the kidneys do?
answers: Filter waste products from the blood to make urine
questions.explanation: The kidneys filter the blood, removing waste products and excess water to produce urine, which is stored in the bladder.

questions.question: What is an electrical conductor?
answers: A material that does not allow electricity to flow
A material that stores electrical charge
A material that allows electricity to flow through it easily
questions.explanation: Conductors, such as metals like copper and aluminium, allow electricity to flow freely. This is why wires are made of metal.

questions.question: How long does Earth take to orbit the Sun?
questions.explanation: Earth orbits the Sun in approximately 365 days, which is one year. The extra quarter day each year gives us leap years every 4 years.

questions.question: Which gas makes up most of the air we breathe?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen makes up about 78% of the air. Oxygen makes up about 21%, with the rest being other gases including CO2.

questions.question: What is an adaptation?
answers: When an animal moves to a new habitat
A feature that helps a living thing survive in its environment
The process of an animal becoming extinct
When two species compete for the same food
questions.explanation: Adaptations are features that help organisms survive. A polar bear's thick white fur is an adaptation to cold, snowy habitats.

questions.question: What is the function of chlorophyll in plants?
answers: It transports water from the roots
questions.explanation: Chlorophyll is the green pigment in leaves that absorbs sunlight. This light energy powers the process of photosynthesis.

questions.question: What is the difference between a series and a parallel circuit?
answers: Series uses AC; parallel uses DC
In series, components share one path; in parallel, components have separate paths
Series has more components than parallel
Parallel circuits only work with batteries
questions.explanation: In a series circuit all components share one loop. In parallel, each component has its own loop — a break in one branch doesn't stop others.

questions.question: What is the name for the process when a solid changes directly to a gas without becoming liquid first?
questions.explanation: Sublimation is when a solid changes directly into a gas. Dry ice (solid CO2) sublimates at room temperature.
